summary: Comprehensive backend pytest suite for Rama Bazaar 1.0 — 47/48 tests passed (97.9%). All auth, visitor, exhibitor, roster, attendance, sponsor-ads, admin stats, settings, member, upload flows verified end-to-end (including Create→GET, Update→GET, Delete→GET data persistence checks). One failure: QR PNG size below 20KB WhatsApp shareability threshold required by spec.

minor.issue: QR PNG generated is ~9.8KB. Spec requires >20KB for reliable WhatsApp media-message shareability (BizChat tends to thumbnail/skip very small images). Current qrcode.QRCode config (box_size=20, border=4, ERROR_CORRECT_H, optimize=False) yields ~512x512 PNG ~10KB. Increase box_size to ~40 (or add a branded canvas/letterhead background) to push PNG above 20KB; alternatively use PIL to embed QR in a larger branded image.

action_items: Increase generated QR PNG size to >20KB for WhatsApp delivery. Suggested fix in /app/backend/server.py @ visitor_qr_image: bump box_size from 20 to ~40, or composite QR onto a branded background canvas with event header/footer. After fix, re-run TestVisitors::test_visitor_qr_png_size.
critical_code_review_comments: JWT_SECRET present in .env (good); JWT 7-day expiry is OK for PWA but consider refresh-token pattern for prod.

CORS_ORIGINS='*' with allow_credentials=True is technically allowed by Starlette but browsers will reject credentialed requests — fine here since JWT is in Authorization header (not cookies). Document this so it isn't switched to httpOnly cookies later.
/api/upload has no auth — anyone can upload files up to 8MB. Acceptable for visitor/exhibitor self-registration flows but consider rate limiting.
/api/sponsor-ads/{id}/impression and /click are unauthenticated and unprotected — vulnerable to counter spam. Consider IP-based rate limit or session-scoped impression dedupe.
admin_update_exhibitor accepts arbitrary payload dict (only strips id/password_hash). Could allow setting unexpected fields (e.g., 'mobile') — consider Pydantic model.
visitor_register returns the full existing doc on duplicate including qr_id — acceptable since users need to retrieve QR, but consider explicit retrieve endpoint use.
server.py is 729 lines — close to the 700-line guideline; consider splitting into routers (auth, visitors, exhibitors, admin, sponsors).
bcrypt hashes verified to start with $2b$ (default bcrypt.gensalt). Good.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@admin.com / Admin@123 (verified working). Exhibitor created dynamically per test run.
seed_data_creation: Test data is created with TEST_ prefix and cleaned up in session-scoped fixture (test exhibitor, visitors, allowed_members with 'TEST'/'bulk' notes are deleted after run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_rama_bazaar.py — 48 tests covering all spec endpoints. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_rama_bazaar.py -v. Only known failure is QR PNG size <20KB. All auth/CRUD/roster/attendance/sponsor flows pass with full data-persistence verification (Create→GET, Update→GET, Delete→404). BizChat WhatsApp send is unconfigured (returns {skipped:true}) — not tested in this run since env vars empty.

rca_of_qr_size_issue.bug: QR PNG is 9836 bytes; spec requires >20KB for WhatsApp shareability.
rca_of_qr_size_issue.reproduction: 1) POST /api/visitors/register with any valid mobile -> obtain qr_id. 2) GET /api/visitors/qr/{qr_id}.png -> check Content-Length. Got ~9.8KB.
rca_of_qr_size_issue.root_cause: In server.py visitor_qr_image: qrcode.QRCode(box_size=20, border=4) produces ~512x512px PNG which compresses to ~10KB. Although optimize=False is set, PNG is still small because the image is mostly two-color and highly compressible.
rca_of_qr_size_issue.mitigation: Option A: increase box_size to 40 (yields ~1024x1024, ~25-35KB). Option B (preferred): use PIL to composite QR onto a larger branded poster (event name, visitor name, venue) — pushes file size well above 20KB and improves UX in WhatsApp preview.
